evidence: maybeSpeakErrorFallback (voice-agent-session.ts:993-1007) only fires for pkt.component === 'llm' (line 997) and injects make.injectMessage(`${contextId}:error-fallback`, ...). handleInjectMessage (line 1018-1022) routes it through make.llmDelta + make.llmDone, i.e. the NORMAL TTS path. There is NO TTS health gate anywhere (grep for tts health/down/available returns nothing). The code COMMENT at lines 995-996 explicitly scopes this out: 'a TTS/STT failure can't reliably use the same TTS path for a fallback (that needs canned audio / a clarification prompt — out of scope)'. So if LLM fails AND TTS is simultaneously down, the injected fallback produces zero audio = dead air. HOWEVER it is not fully silent: VAQI missed-response timer is armed on user speech-ended keyed to the user-turn contextId (startVaqiMissedResponseTimer, line 673) and only cleared on first tts.audio (clearVaqiIfContext inside handleTtsAudio first-audio block, line 870). With no tts.audio it fires after vaqiMissedResponseMs (default 4000ms) and pushes make.metric(cid,'vaqi.missed_response',...) (util.ts:129). The TTS-down condition also surfaces its own tts.error. So the dead air is OBSERVED, just not recovered. The stall watchdog does NOT catch this: armTtsStallTimer is only called from handleTtsAudio (line 883, comment line 128 'Armed only after the first tts.audio') and its callback bails on '!ttsPlayout.isActive(cid)' (util.ts:148), so a TTS that emits zero packets is never watched.
real_fix: Real but narrow — requires SIMULTANEOUS LLM failure and TTS down, and the dead air is already surfaced via the vaqi.missed_response metric and the tts.error packet (not invisible to operators). The 'never fail silently' guarantee holds for the common single-component-failure case; the double failure is explicitly documented as out of scope (canned audio path) at line 996. If the team wants to close the gap: emit a non-audio escalation when fallbackInjectedContexts has the turn but no tts.audio arrives within a window (i.e. arm a one-shot watchdog at injection time in maybeSpeakErrorFallback / handleInjectMessage for fallback-suffixed contexts, since the existing TtsStall watchdog never arms without a first audio packet). A canned/pre-synthesized audio path is the more complete fix but is a larger feature, correctly deferred. Do not gate the existing LLM fallback on a TTS health signal that does not exist today.
files: packages/voice/src/voice-agent-session.ts:993-1007 (maybeSpeakErrorFallback), :1018-1022 (handleInjectMessage), :883 + packages/voice/src/voice-agent-session-util.ts:139-162 (armTtsStallTimer arms only after first tts.audio), :117-131 (VAQI metric-only, no recovery)
